issues.description: The duplicate diagnostic message embeds internal Vec indices ('indices 0 and 1') that are meaningless to end users — they have no mapping to a source location. The earlier duplicate-entity pre-pass at crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:3571-3670 produces a proper two-label diagnostic anchored at both definition spans. Because TopologyTemplate has no span field (see lib.rs:178-202), this function cannot produce the same quality. If this diagnostic ever fires, users will see 'duplicate template name A: indices 0 and 1' with no actionable context.
issues.suggested_fix: Either (a) prefix the message with 'internal compiler error:' so it is clearly distinguishable from the real user-facing duplicate diagnostic and omit the raw indices, or (b) store a span on TopologyTemplate during compile_entity so this pass can attach proper labels. Option (a) is lower-cost and matches the defensive-only nature of the check.

issues.description: This check is redundant with the existing pre-pass at crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:3571-3670 (seen_entity_names) which already rejects duplicate entity names and skips compiling the second definition (lib.rs:3899 is_first_def guard). In normal operation, detect_recursive_structures can never see duplicates. When this branch fires, it indicates a regression in the pre-pass — i.e., an internal compiler bug, not a user error. Emitting a Severity::Error diagnostic here will look like a real compile error and mask the underlying pre-pass regression.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a debug_assert! for duplicate absence in addition to the diagnostic, or (better) combine: debug_assert!(prev_idx is None, 'pre-pass invariant violated: duplicate reached SCC'); then fall back to a clearly-labeled internal-compiler-error diagnostic in release. Consistent with reconstruct_scc_cycle's debug_assert pattern on line 218.

issues.description: The test duplicate_template_names_emits_diagnostic is too weak. It only asserts that some diagnostic message contains the substring 'duplicate', with no check on: (1) severity (Error vs Warning), (2) how many diagnostics fire for N duplicates, (3) that the function still returns consistent cyclic_sccs results in the face of duplicates, (4) that the 'first wins' policy is actually applied (the kept index matches the first definition). It also doesn't cover the 3+ duplicate case, which under the current code produces (N-1) separate pairwise diagnostics — likely not intended.
issues.suggested_fix: Strengthen the test: assert severity == Error, assert exactly one duplicate diagnostic for the 2-template case, add a 3-duplicate case and assert the expected diagnostic count, and assert that the returned cyclic_sccs / in_cycle flags are consistent (e.g., no panic, adjacency still well-formed). Consider also testing that duplicate + self-reference in the original still produces exactly one cycle warning.

issues.description: The manual get-then-insert pattern can be expressed more idiomatically with HashMap::entry, which avoids the double lookup and makes the 'first wins' intent clearer.

Even with this defensive check, the downstream behavior when duplicates slip through is subtly wrong: the duplicate template at index 1 has its adjacency built using name_to_idx which maps its self-references to index 0 (not 1). So node 1's outgoing self-edges point at node 0, and Tarjan's won't tag index 1 as recursive even if the original is. This is acceptable as a degraded-but-safe fallback, but it's not documented in the comment on line 16-17, which only mentions 'corruption of the adjacency graph' generally. Future readers may assume the defensive check fully preserves correctness.
issues.suggested_fix: Expand the comment: 'Use explicit insertion so duplicates are reported instead of silently overwriting. Note that when duplicates slip through, only the first index is reachable by name-based lookup, so duplicate templates' recursive tagging may be incorrect — this is acceptable because the pre-pass (seen_entity_names in lib.rs) prevents duplicates from reaching this point in normal operation.'

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: The diff correctly addresses a silent-corruption hazard in detect_recursive_structures by making duplicate template names in the HashMap construction explicit and noisy. The change is functionally safe and accompanied by a test. However, the defensive check is redundant with the earlier seen_entity_names pre-pass (lib.rs:3571-3670) which already enforces uniqueness before compile_entity runs — so if this code ever fires, it indicates an internal compiler bug, not a user error. Issues: (1) the diagnostic exposes raw Vec indices with no source location, unlike the proper span-labeled diagnostic produced by the pre-pass; (2) severity Error will look like a user-facing compile error but really signals a pre-pass regression — consider debug_assert or an 'internal compiler error' prefix; (3) the unit test only asserts substring match on 'duplicate' without checking severity, count, or that downstream results remain consistent; (4) HashMap::entry() would express first-wins more idiomatically; (5) the comment understates the degraded behavior when duplicates do slip through (node 1's self-refs point at node 0, so duplicates won't be tagged recursive correctly). All findings are suggestions — nothing blocks within the scope of this defensive hardening task.
